summarization.a: The figure shows a top-view schematic of an ideal HfO₂ structure placed on a Si/SiO₂ substrate. Different symbols are used to mark atom types and their layer positions. Silicon atoms appear only in the first layer, while hafnium atoms are placed in alternating positions depending on whether the layer number is even or odd. Ionic oxygen positions are also marked to show local bonding sites. The dashed square highlights one repeating two-dimensional cell where atom types and positions are clearly defined. This layout is used to remove confusion when building models or simulations of HfO₂ growth on silicon.

a.question: What does the dashed square represent in the figure?
According to figure, which atomic species appears only in the k = 1 layer?
a.answer: Silicon atoms (represented by inverted white triangles) appear exclusively in the k = 1 layer.

a.question: What is this type of diagram mainly used for?
a.answer:
-Building simulation input structures
, -Defining atomic positions clearly
, -Avoiding ambiguity in lattice models

a.question: How does the highlighted unit cell help in understanding the full structure?
a.answer: The highlighted unit cell shows the smallest repeating part of the lattice. Once this cell is defined, the full structure can be built by repeating it in two dimensions. This makes the structure easier to understand, copy, and use in calculations without redefining every atom manually.
